name: 1st Ed Credit Union
description_short: Pennsylvania-based credit union serving school employees, students, and partner company employees across five counties with competitive loan rates and surcharge-free ATM access.
description_long:
1st Ed Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union operating in Pennsylvania with physical branches in Chambersburg, Greencastle, and Hanover. The organization was established to provide quality financial services and personal attention to members' financial stability, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks.

1st Ed offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including share accounts (savings), certificates (CDs at 3.60% APY), auto loans (as low as 4.99% APR), mortgages, home equity lines of credit (4.25% APR), personal loans (up to 60 months at 11.15%), and vehicle loans. Members gain access to over 32,000 surcharge-free ATMs through the credit union network, eliminating out-of-network fees. The credit union actively promotes membership benefits including a refer-a-friend program ($25 per referral) and youth savings incentives ($50 bonus for young savers).

What distinguishes 1st Ed is its membership eligibility structure—the credit union specifically targets school employees and students in Adams, Cumberland, Franklin, Fulton, and York counties, plus employees of partner companies in the region and their families. This field-of-membership model creates a tight-knit community focus. The organization maintains active community engagement through annual meetings, financial education content (debt management, money habits for kids, smart goal-setting for educators), and promotional campaigns. They also offer convenient loan payment processing and support member financial wellness through educational resources.

As a credit union, 1st Ed provides NCUA insurance protection on member deposits (similar to FDIC insurance at banks). The competitive rates advertised (particularly the 4.99% auto loan rate) are attractive for the market. However, membership eligibility is restricted to specific employee groups and geographic regions—this is not an open-membership institution. The website lacks detailed information about minimum deposits, account fees, or comprehensive rate sheets for all products, which may require direct contact with branches for complete pricing transparency.
services: Financial education and money management resources
pros: Access to 32,000+ surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through credit union network
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.99% APR
Refer-a-friend program offering $25 bonuses to both referrer and new member
Certificate special at 3.60% APY available for savers
Financial education resources covering debt management, kids' money habits, and teacher-specific financial planning
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits benefit members rather than shareholders
Three convenient locations in regional Pennsylvania communities
cons: Membership eligibility is restricted to school employees, students, and partner company employees in specific counties—not open to the general public
Limited branch network (only 3 locations) compared to large banks, may be inconvenient for members outside service areas
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, minimum deposit requirements, and complete rate transparency for all products
Personal loan rates at 11.15% are higher than some competing lenders, though typical for credit unions
No mention of online account opening or digital banking features on homepage, unclear if available
best_for: School employees (teachers, administrators, staff) in Pennsylvania's Adams, Cumberland, Franklin, Fulton, or York counties
Students attending schools in the credit union's service area seeking youth savings accounts
Employees of partner companies within the region looking for member-owned financial institution with community focus
Auto loan borrowers prioritizing rates below 5% APR with surcharge-free ATM access
diagnosis: 1st Ed Credit Union is best for school employees, students, and partner company staff in central Pennsylvania seeking competitive loan rates and member-owned financial services with community focus. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is field-restricted—you must qualify as a school employee, student, or partner company employee in specific counties; this is not a general-access credit union.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: 1st Ed Credit Union — NCUA-insured credit union in Chambersburg, PA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
